title: Cannot connect to services on host from a container on bridge network
body:
### Issue Description

Since updating Podman from version 4.9.4 to 5.2.2 my containers on the bridge network can't reach any services on the host network anymore. It is basically the same issue as #22653, but perhaps I should start questioning my perception or intelligence, if not both, because it is closed and  I was unable to find a solution for bridge network.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start alpine container using the bridge (default) network mode:
`podman container run -it alpine /bin/sh`
2. Install curl:
`apk update && apk upgrade && apk add curl`
3. Try the webpage running on host (it is not public):
`curl -vk https://example.int/`

### Describe the results you received

```
* Host example.int:443 was resolved.
* IPv6: (none)
* IPv4: 172.21.[REDACTED]
*   Trying 172.21.[REDACTED]:443...
* connect to 172.21.[REDACTED] port 443 from 172.21.[REDACTED] port 33760 failed: Connection refused
* Failed to connect to example.int port 443 after 1 ms: Could not connect to server
* closing connection #0
curl: (7) Failed to connect to example.int port 443 after 1 ms: Could not connect to server
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
/ # curl -vk https://example.int/
* Host example.int:443 was resolved.
* IPv6: (none)
* IPv4: 172.21.[REDACTED]
* Trying 172.21.[REDACTED]:443...
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Server is a VM running on RHEL 9.5

### Additional information

I noticed that on the old version in the `/etc/hosts` file IP address leading to the container itself is the IP of a container on the bridge network (the default one within `10.88.0.0/16`), while on the new version is the IP of the host. Tried setting static IP using `--ip` argument and also using `--no-hosts` argument but neither did help. Also, using `host` or `slirp4netns` network mode resolves the issue, but I need my containers on the bridge network.

comments.body:
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24613#issuecomment-2486672230

As the exact same ip is copied by pasta into the rootless-netns you cannot use it to connect to the host. You will have to use another ip or host.containers.internal.
I think I understand now. Thanks.
